name: SQL Injection Response
description: Incident response playbook for SQL injection attacks targeting web applications and APIs. Covers detection, containment, remediation, and hardening.

steps.title: Review WAF & Application Logs
steps.description: Examine Web Application Firewall logs and application server access logs for SQL injection patterns (UNION SELECT, OR 1=1, encoded payloads, error-based injection signatures).

steps.title: Identify Affected Endpoints
steps.description: Map all endpoints targeted by the injection attempts. Determine which received successful (200/500) responses vs blocked requests.
commands: awk '{print $7}' access.log | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -20

steps.title: Assess Data Exposure
steps.description: Determine if the attacker successfully extracted data. Check for abnormal response sizes, database query logs showing unauthorized SELECTs, and any data exfiltration indicators.
commands: SELECT * FROM mysql.general_log WHERE command_type='Query' AND argument LIKE '%UNION%' ORDER BY event_time DESC;

steps.title: Block Source IP Addresses
steps.description: Add attacker IP addresses to the firewall and WAF block lists immediately.
commands: az network nsg rule create --name BlockAttacker --priority 100 --source-address-prefixes <ATTACKER_IP> --access Deny

steps.title: Enable Strict WAF Rules
steps.description: Escalate WAF to blocking mode for SQL injection rule sets. Enable paranoia level 2+ on ModSecurity or equivalent.

steps.title: Isolate Affected Database
steps.description: If data compromise is confirmed, isolate the affected database from the application tier. Switch to read-only mode or failover to clean replica.

steps.title: Patch Vulnerable Code
steps.description: Identify and fix the vulnerable code. Replace string concatenation with parameterised queries / prepared statements.

steps.title: Rotate Database Credentials
steps.description: Rotate all database credentials, API keys, and connection strings that may have been exposed.

steps.title: Deploy Input Validation
steps.description: Add server-side input validation and output encoding across all affected endpoints. Implement allowlisting for expected parameter formats.

steps.title: Restore from Clean Backup
steps.description: If data was modified or deleted, restore the database from the last known clean backup taken before the attack.

steps.title: Verify Data Integrity
steps.description: Run integrity checks against known-good checksums. Verify row counts and critical data fields match expected values.

steps.title: Re-enable Services
steps.description: Gradually re-enable services with enhanced monitoring. Watch for repeat attack attempts from the same or new sources.

steps.title: Conduct Post-Incident Review
steps.description: Hold a blameless post-mortem within 48 hours. Document the timeline, root cause, impact, and response effectiveness.

steps.title: Update Detection Rules
steps.description: Improve WAF rules and SIEM detection queries based on the specific attack patterns observed in this incident.

steps.title: Schedule Security Testing
steps.description: Commission a penetration test focused on SQL injection across all web-facing applications. Add SQLi test cases to the CI/CD pipeline.

name: Phishing Attack Response
description: Incident response playbook for phishing and social engineering attacks including credential harvesting, BEC (Business Email Compromise), and malicious attachments.

steps.title: Analyse the Phishing Email
steps.description: Examine email headers (SPF, DKIM, DMARC results), sender domain age, embedded URLs, and attachment hashes. Check for typosquatting and lookalike domains.

steps.title: Identify Affected Users
steps.description: Search email logs to determine all recipients. Check if any users clicked the link or opened the attachment using email gateway tracking and endpoint telemetry.

steps.title: Check for Credential Compromise
steps.description: If the phishing page harvested credentials, check authentication logs for successful logins from unexpected locations or new devices for affected users.

steps.title: Block Malicious URLs & Domains
steps.description: Add the phishing URLs and sender domains to the block list across email gateway, web proxy, and DNS filtering.

steps.title: Force Password Reset for Affected Users
steps.description: Immediately reset passwords and revoke active sessions for any user who entered credentials on the phishing page.

steps.title: Quarantine the Phishing Email
steps.description: Remove the phishing email from all mailboxes that received it, including those who haven't opened it yet.

steps.title: Scan Endpoints for Malware
steps.description: If the phishing email contained a malicious attachment, run full endpoint scans on all machines where the attachment was opened.

steps.title: Review and Revoke OAuth Tokens
steps.description: Check if the attacker obtained OAuth consent grants. Revoke any suspicious app permissions granted during the phishing attack.

steps.title: Enable MFA for Affected Accounts
steps.description: Ensure all affected accounts have multi-factor authentication enabled. Consider phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2, passkeys).

steps.title: Monitor for Follow-up Attacks
steps.description: Set up enhanced monitoring for affected user accounts and the attacker infrastructure for 30 days post-incident.

steps.title: Update Email Security Policies
steps.description: Review and tighten SPF, DKIM, and DMARC policies. Consider implementing BIMI for brand protection.

steps.title: Conduct Phishing Awareness Training
steps.description: Run targeted phishing simulation for affected department. Update security awareness training with this incident as a case study.

name: Brute Force Attack Response
description: Incident response playbook for brute force and credential stuffing attacks against SSH, RDP, web applications, and Active Directory services.

steps.title: Analyse Authentication Logs
steps.description: Review failed authentication logs to determine the scope: number of attempts, targeted accounts, source IPs, and time window.
commands: journalctl -u sshd | grep 'Failed password' | awk '{print $11}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn

steps.title: Check for Successful Compromise
steps.description: Determine if any brute-forced account was successfully compromised by correlating failed attempts with subsequent successful logins from the same source.

steps.title: Identify Attack Type
steps.description: Distinguish between simple brute force (one account, many passwords), password spraying (many accounts, few passwords), and credential stuffing (known credential pairs).

steps.title: Block Attacking IPs
steps.description: Block the source IP addresses at the firewall and add them to the fail2ban or equivalent automated blocking system.

steps.title: Lock Targeted Accounts
steps.description: Temporarily lock any accounts that were specifically targeted, especially privileged accounts (root, Administrator, service accounts).

steps.title: Enforce Strong Password Policy
steps.description: Ensure all targeted accounts meet minimum password complexity requirements. Force password changes for any accounts with weak or commonly-used passwords.

steps.title: Implement Rate Limiting
steps.description: Configure rate limiting and progressive delays on all authentication endpoints. Set account lockout thresholds.

steps.title: Unlock & Monitor Accounts
steps.description: Unlock previously locked accounts after password resets are confirmed. Set up enhanced monitoring for these accounts.

steps.title: Deploy MFA
steps.description: Enable multi-factor authentication on all accounts targeted in the attack, prioritising privileged accounts and remote access services.

steps.title: Review Access Architecture
steps.description: Evaluate whether SSH/RDP should be exposed to the internet. Consider bastion hosts, VPN-only access, or zero-trust network access.

steps.title: Implement Threat Intelligence Feeds
steps.description: Subscribe to IP reputation feeds and integrate with firewall/IDS to proactively block known malicious sources.

name: Malware Infection Response
description: Incident response playbook for malware infections including trojans, ransomware, C2 beacons, and fileless malware on endpoints and servers.

steps.title: Analyse Malware Indicators
steps.description: Collect file hashes, process names, network IOCs (C2 domains/IPs), and registry modifications. Check against threat intelligence databases.

steps.title: Determine Infection Vector
steps.description: Trace back how the malware entered the environment: phishing email, drive-by download, USB, supply chain, or lateral movement from another compromised host.

steps.title: Scope the Infection
steps.description: Search across all endpoints for the same IOCs to determine the full blast radius of the infection.

steps.title: Isolate Infected Endpoints
steps.description: Network-isolate all confirmed infected machines using EDR or network segmentation. Maintain management access for investigation.

steps.title: Block C2 Communications
steps.description: Add all identified Command & Control domains and IPs to DNS sinkhole and firewall block lists to prevent data exfiltration.

steps.title: Preserve Forensic Evidence
steps.description: Capture memory dumps and disk images from infected systems before remediation for forensic analysis.

steps.title: Remove Malware & Persistence
steps.description: Remove malware binaries, scheduled tasks, registry keys, and any other persistence mechanisms identified during analysis.

steps.title: Patch Exploitation Vector
steps.description: If malware entered via a software vulnerability, apply the relevant security patches immediately across all systems.

steps.title: Rebuild Compromised Systems
steps.description: For critical infections, reimage affected systems from known-good base images rather than attempting in-place cleanup.

steps.title: Restore Data & Validate
steps.description: Restore user data from clean backups. Scan all restored files to ensure no reinfection from backup data.

steps.title: Update Detection Signatures
steps.description: Add all IOCs from this incident to SIEM detection rules, EDR custom indicators, and email gateway block lists.

steps.title: Review Endpoint Hardening
steps.description: Assess application allowlisting, script execution policies, and macro restrictions. Implement recommendations from the incident analysis.

name: DDoS Attack Response
description: Incident response playbook for Distributed Denial of Service attacks including SYN floods, HTTP floods, DNS amplification, and application-layer attacks.

steps.title: Confirm DDoS vs Legitimate Traffic
steps.description: Analyse traffic patterns to distinguish between a DDoS attack and legitimate traffic spikes (marketing campaign, viral content). Check source IP diversity, request patterns, and protocol distribution.
commands: netstat -an | awk '{print $5}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -20

steps.title: Classify Attack Type
steps.description: Determine if the attack is volumetric (bandwidth exhaustion), protocol-based (SYN/ACK flood), or application-layer (HTTP flood, slowloris).

steps.title: Assess Business Impact
steps.description: Determine which services are affected, current uptime status, and customer impact. Check SLA obligations and escalation triggers.

steps.title: Activate DDoS Mitigation Service
steps.description: Enable cloud-based DDoS protection or reroute traffic through scrubbing centres. Engage ISP upstream filtering if needed.

steps.title: Apply Rate Limiting & Geo-Blocking
steps.description: Implement aggressive rate limiting on affected endpoints. If attack traffic is geographically concentrated, apply temporary geo-blocking rules.

steps.title: Scale Infrastructure
steps.description: If cloud-hosted, auto-scale backend capacity to absorb residual attack traffic that passes through mitigation.

steps.title: Fine-Tune Filtering Rules
steps.description: Analyse the scrubbed traffic to refine filtering rules. Identify and block traffic patterns unique to the attack without impacting legitimate users.

steps.title: Blackhole Attacking ASNs
steps.description: If the attack originates primarily from specific ASNs or botnets, work with your ISP to implement BGP blackhole routing for those networks.

steps.title: Verify Service Restoration
steps.description: Confirm all services are fully operational. Check response times, error rates, and user experience from multiple geographic locations.

steps.title: Communicate with Stakeholders
steps.description: Update the status page. Notify customers and stakeholders about the resolution. Provide a preliminary timeline and impact summary.

steps.title: Review DDoS Resilience Architecture
steps.description: Evaluate CDN coverage, anycast deployment, auto-scaling policies, and DDoS protection service effectiveness. Identify gaps exposed by the attack.

steps.title: Update DDoS Response Runbook
steps.description: Document specific commands, contacts, and procedures that were effective. Update ISP and DDoS provider escalation contacts.

name: Insider Threat Response
description: Incident response playbook for insider threat incidents including data exfiltration by employees, privilege abuse, and unauthorized access to sensitive systems.

steps.title: Review DLP & UEBA Alerts
steps.description: Examine Data Loss Prevention alerts and User & Entity Behavior Analytics anomalies. Look for unusual data access patterns, off-hours activity, and large data transfers.

steps.title: Correlate User Activity
steps.description: Build a timeline of the suspect's activities across all systems: file access, email, cloud storage, VPN, physical access logs, and print logs.

steps.title: Engage Legal & HR
steps.description: Before taking containment actions, consult with Legal and HR to ensure the investigation follows proper procedures and preserves evidence admissibility.

steps.title: Restrict Access Quietly
steps.description: Reduce the suspect's access privileges without alerting them if the investigation is ongoing. Remove access to sensitive data stores and limit VPN/remote access.

steps.title: Enable Enhanced Monitoring
steps.description: Deploy additional monitoring on the suspect's accounts and endpoints: keylogging (if legally permitted), screen recording, file access auditing, and email DLP scanning.

steps.title: Block Data Exfiltration Channels
steps.description: Block personal cloud storage, USB mass storage, personal email, and other data exfiltration channels for the suspect's accounts.

steps.title: Disable Accounts & Access
steps.description: Upon decision to act, immediately disable all user accounts, revoke VPN certificates, badge access, and recover any company devices.

steps.title: Audit Data Exposure
steps.description: Determine exactly what data was accessed, copied, or exfiltrated. Assess regulatory notification requirements (GDPR, HIPAA, PCI-DSS).

steps.title: Rotate Shared Credentials
steps.description: Change all shared credentials, API keys, and service accounts that the insider had access to.

steps.title: Review Access Controls
steps.description: Implement least-privilege access reviews for all similar roles. Ensure separation of duties prevents a single user from accessing and exfiltrating sensitive data.

steps.title: Implement Behavioural Analytics
steps.description: Deploy or tune UEBA solutions to detect future insider threats earlier. Establish baseline behaviour profiles for privileged users.

steps.title: Update Insider Threat Programme
steps.description: Review and update the insider threat programme including policies, detection capabilities, investigation procedures, and employee awareness.
